balanceOf(address,uint256).details: Returns the value of tokens of token type `id` owned by `account`. Requirements: - `account` cannot be the zero address.

balanceOfBatch(address[],uint256[]).details: xref:ROOT:erc1155.adoc#batch-operations[Batched] version of {balanceOf}. Requirements: - `accounts` and `ids` must have the same length.

endBounty((uint128,uint128,address,uint4.details: uncappedBountyFraction = block.timestamp > targetEndTimestamp + gracePeriod ? (block.timestamp - targetEndTimestamp - gracePeriod) * bountyFractionIncreasePerSecond : 0;bountyFraction = max(uncappedBountyFraction, maxBountyFraction)bounty = bountyFraction * collateral / 10**18;

returns._0: If any of the pools are already ended, then returns 0 for all bounties. Otherwise returns each bounty, quoted in the `payoutToken` of the pool.

returns._0: True if the order has been cancelled, false otherwise
PoolState struct that matched the input hash

params.longPoolParams: The pools to redeem long shares from.
params.shortPoolParams: The pools to redeem short shares from.

safeBatchTransferFrom(address,address,ui.details: xref:ROOT:erc1155.adoc#batch-operations[Batched] version of {safeTransferFrom}. WARNING: This function can potentially allow a reentrancy attack when transferring tokens to an untrusted contract, when invoking {onERC1155BatchReceived} on the receiver. Ensure to follow the checks-effects-interactions pattern and consider employing reentrancy guards when interacting with untrusted contracts. Emits either a {TransferSingle} or a {TransferBatch} event, depending on the length of the array arguments. Requirements: - `ids` and `values` must have the same length. - If `to` refers to a smart contract, it must implement {IERC1155Receiver-onERC1155BatchReceived} and return the acceptance magic value.

safeTransferFrom(address,address,uint256.details: Transfers a `value` amount of tokens of type `id` from `from` to `to`. WARNING: This function can potentially allow a reentrancy attack when transferring tokens to an untrusted contract, when invoking {onERC1155Received} on the receiver. Ensure to follow the checks-effects-interactions pattern and consider employing reentrancy guards when interacting with untrusted contracts. Emits a {TransferSingle} event. Requirements: - `to` cannot be the zero address. - If the caller is not `from`, it must have been approved to spend ``from``'s tokens via {setApprovalForAll}. - `from` must have a balance of tokens of type `id` of at least `value` amount. - If `to` refers to a smart contract, it must implement {IERC1155Receiver-onERC1155Received} and return the acceptance magic value.

setApprovalForAll(address,bool).details: Grants or revokes permission to `operator` to transfer the caller's tokens, according to `approved`, Emits an {ApprovalForAll} event. Requirements: - `operator` cannot be the caller.

This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

bountyFractionIncreasePerSecond().notice: The rate at which the bounty as a fraction of collateral increases per second

bountyGracePeriod().notice: The grace period, in seconds, after the pool's target start & end times during which no bounties are paid

cancelOrders((address,address,uint48,add.notice: Cancels the given orders.The caller must be the maker of each order.

collateralRefund((uint128,uint128,addres.notice: Refunds mint collateral to the caller.The caller must have approved this contract to transfer their long and short shares.

collateralizedMint((uint128,uint128,addr.notice: Takes collateral from the caller, equal to the maximum payout:         (cap - floor) * shares         denominated in `SilicaPool.payoutToken`The caller must have approved this contract to transfer `SilicaPool.payoutToken`.

endBounty((uint128,uint128,address,uint4.notice: View function to estimate bounty for timely finalization of index tracking.

endPools((uint128,uint128,address,uint48.notice: Records the ending `ISilicaIndex` state for any of         the specified pools which have not already been ended.         Caller will be paid a bounty for each pool which was not         already ended if called after the grace period.Can only be called after pool's target end time.

fillFeeBps().notice: The fee, in basis points, for minting long and short shares

fillOrders((address,address,uint48,addre.notice: Transfers all `offeredLongShares`, `offeredUpfrontAmount`,         `requestedLongShares`, `requestedUpfrontAmount` from/to         the appropriate parties         (`offered` should go from `order.maker` to `msg.sender`,         `requested` should go from `msg.sender` to `order.maker`).         If `order.taker != 0x0` the order is only fillable by `order.taker`.         This function SHOULD revert if any fill fails.         `UpfrontAmount`s SHOULD be transferred before any `LongShares` are minted,         to reduce the required allowance for minting `LongShares`.The caller must have approved this contract to transfer `requestedUpfrontToken`.If the order is private, the caller must be the taker.The input arrays must match in length.

maxBountyFraction().notice: The maximum bounty, as a fraction of the pool's collateral, that can be paid out

maxCollateralRefund((uint128,uint128,add.notice: Refunds mint collateral to the caller from the given pool.The caller must have approved this contract to transfer long and short shares.

orderCancelled(bytes32).notice: Indicates if a given order has been cancelled

pause().notice: Pause the protocol. Only callable by owner

poolState(bytes32).notice: Returns PoolState struct that matched the input hash

redeem((uint128,uint128,address,uint48,u.notice: Redeems shares for the payout token.The caller must have approved this contract to transfer their long and short shares.

startBounty((uint128,uint128,address,uin.notice: View function to estimate bounty for timely initialization of index tracking.

startPools((uint128,uint128,address,uint.notice: Records the starting `ISilicaIndex` state for any of         the specified pools which have not already been started.         Caller will be paid a bounty for each pool which was not         already started if called after the grace period.Can only be called after pool's target start time.

treasuryAddress().notice: The address which receives the mint fees

documentation.text:
@notice !TRADE OFFER!
         i receive: requested long shares, requested upfront amount.
         you receive: offered long shares, offered upfront amount.
         `SilicaOrder` may not be used to offer/request short shares,
         since you can offer short shares by requesting long shares,
         and you can request short shares by offering long shares.
 @custom:example To sell stETH yield for upfront USDC, set
                 `offeredIndex` to stETH index and
                 `requestedUpfrontToken` to USDC.
                 Set `requestedIndex` and `offeredUpfrontToken` to 0x0.
 @custom:example To buy stETH yield with upfront USDC, set
                 `requestedIndex` to stETH index
                 and `offeredUpfrontToken` to USDC.
                 Set `offeredIndex` and `requestedUpfrontToken` to 0x0.
 @custom:example To do a "float-to-float" trade, set both `offeredIndex`
                 and `requestedIndex`. If the `offeredLongShares` is a greater
                 exposure than the `requestedLongShares`, then the
                 `requestedUpfrontAmount` should compensate, and vice versa.
 @custom:example To "deleverage", i.e. sell the full balance change without
                 subtracting the `floor`: set both `offeredIndex`
                 and `offeredUpfrontToken`. Set `offeredUpfrontAmount` to
                 `offeredfloor * offeredLongShares`.
 @custom:example For a deleveraged float-to-float trade, set all 4 fields:
                 `offeredIndex`, `offeredUpfrontToken`,
                 `requestedIndex`, `requestedUpfrontToken`.
@notice Domain separator for EIP-712.

@notice The address which receives the mint fees

@notice The rate at which the bounty as a fraction of collateral increases per second
